question: Hello
answer: Hello and welcome to IN2 Branding. We craft premium, retail-inspired branded gifts, curated gift sets and scented products for companies that want to make a lasting impression. How can I help you today — are you exploring products, techniques, or planning a specific gifting project?

question: What does IN2 Branding do?
answer: IN2 Branding is a Polish company specialising in premium, high-quality branded marketing products, curated gift sets, co-branding and our own line of scented products (IN2 Scented Art). We deliberately reject cheap disposable gadgets — the kind of 'anti-advertising' that ends up in a drawer — and instead create elegant, retail-worthy pieces your recipients actually keep. We manage everything end to end: sourcing, branding concepts, packaging design, production and logistics.

question: Why do you only work with premium products?
answer: We believe a cheap, disposable gadget is 'anti-advertising' — it quietly tells your recipient how much (or how little) they are valued, and usually ends up in the bin. We work only with premium products, elegant branding techniques and refined packaging, because a beautifully made gift becomes something people keep, use and associate with your brand. Quality over quantity is at the heart of everything we do.

question: How does your end-to-end service work?
answer: We handle the entire journey so you don't have to juggle multiple vendors: sourcing the right premium products, developing branding concepts, designing bespoke packaging, managing production and handling logistics and delivery. You bring the brief and brand guidelines; we bring a curated, on-brand result delivered to your door (or to many doors). Think of us as your fully managed gifting and branded-merchandise partner.

question: How do I get a quote?
answer: The quickest way is to reach out to Aleksandra Stan at Sales@in2branding.pl or +48 509 919 558 with a short brief: what the gift is for, rough quantity, budget range and your timeline. We'll propose curated product and packaging concepts with clear pricing, then refine until it's exactly right. There's no obligation — we love a good brief.

question: How do I contact you?
answer: You can reach Aleksandra Stan directly: email Sales@in2branding.pl or call/message +48 509 919 558. You can also explore our scented collections at www.in2scentedart.com. We're happy to talk through ideas, timelines or a specific brief.

question: What is screen printing?
answer: Screen printing pushes ink through a fine mesh stencil onto the product, one colour at a time. It's ideal for bold, vibrant logos on flat or gently curved surfaces like textiles, bags, notebooks and bottles, and it's very cost-effective at higher volumes — especially for one or two solid colours. For designs with many colours or photographic detail we'll usually recommend digital or UV printing instead.

question: What is pad printing?
answer: Pad printing transfers ink from an engraved plate via a soft silicone pad, which flexes to reach curved, uneven or recessed surfaces. It's the go-to method for pens, tech accessories, drinkware lids and small hard items where a screen can't sit flat. It gives crisp, precise logos and handles multiple colours well on compact areas.

question: What is embroidery best for?
answer: Embroidery stitches your logo directly into the fabric with thread, giving a premium, tactile, long-lasting finish that signals quality. It's perfect for apparel, caps, polos, fleece, tote bags and towels. Very fine detail or gradients don't translate to stitching, so for intricate artwork we'll advise on the best position and level of detail — or suggest a printed alternative.

question: What is laser engraving?
answer: Laser engraving uses a focused beam to etch your logo into the material, revealing a permanent, elegant mark rather than adding colour. It's beautiful and durable on metal, bamboo, wood, leather, glass and coated drinkware — a favourite for premium bottles, pens, tech and desk items. The result is subtle and tonal, so it reads as refined rather than loud, which fits our aesthetic perfectly.

question: What are debossing and embossing?
answer: Debossing presses your logo into the surface to create a tasteful indentation, while embossing raises it up from the surface. Both are branding without ink, giving a discreet, high-end, tactile finish that's especially stunning on leather, faux leather, notebooks and paper packaging. They're a signature choice when you want understated luxury rather than a printed logo.

question: What is doming?
answer: Doming applies a printed logo and seals it under a clear, hardened resin, creating a glossy 3D 'bubble' effect that feels premium and wears well. It's often used for badges, keyrings, USBs and small branded inlays where you want a polished, finished detail. The resin protects the print and adds a tactile, jewel-like quality.

question: What are digital and UV printing?
answer: Digital and UV printing reproduce full-colour, photographic-quality artwork with sharp detail and no per-colour setup, so complex or multi-coloured logos are no problem. UV printing cures ink instantly with UV light, prints on almost any material — including curved bottles and hard surfaces — and gives a crisp, slightly raised, durable finish. These methods are ideal when your design has gradients, many colours or fine detail.

question: What is sublimation printing?
answer: Sublimation uses heat to turn dye into gas that bonds permanently into the fibres of polyester fabrics and polymer-coated items, so the design won't crack, peel or fade and the fabric stays soft. It's brilliant for full-colour, edge-to-edge 'all-over' prints on apparel, sportswear, lanyards, mugs and mouse pads. It works only on suitable (mainly polyester or coated) surfaces, which we'll always confirm for your product.

question: Which branding technique is right for me?
answer: It depends on the material, the artwork and the feel you want — and choosing well is exactly where we add value. As a rule of thumb: engraving and debossing for understated luxury on metal, wood and leather; embroidery for apparel; pad or screen printing for simple logos; and digital or UV printing for full-colour detail. Share your product and logo and we'll recommend the most elegant, durable option.

question: What drinkware do you offer?
answer: Our drinkware range spans insulated stainless-steel bottles and tumblers, travel mugs, thermoses and glass options — chosen for genuine quality and everyday use, not throwaway plastic. Laser engraving gives these pieces a particularly premium, permanent finish. They make excellent standalone gifts or hero items within a curated set.

question: Do you offer branded tech?
answer: Yes — we curate premium tech that people genuinely want to keep: power banks, wireless chargers, quality cables, audio and desk accessories. Because tech tends to be seen and used daily, it's one of the highest-retention gift categories, and we brand it discreetly with pad printing, UV printing or engraving. We steer clients away from cheap, low-capacity gadgets that undermine the brand.

question: What apparel can you brand?
answer: We offer premium apparel — tees, polos, hoodies, fleece, caps, jackets and accessories — in fabrics that feel good and last, so people actually wear them. Depending on the piece and artwork we brand with embroidery for a refined finish, or screen/sublimation printing for bolder, full-colour designs. Ideal for teams, events, onboarding and retail-inspired brand wardrobes.

question: Do you have notebooks and stationery?
answer: Absolutely — premium notebooks, journals, pens and desk pieces that feel considered in the hand. Leather and soft-touch covers look stunning debossed or embossed, while pens suit engraving or pad printing. These are timeless gifts and beautiful anchors for a curated set.

question: Do you offer eco-friendly products?
answer: Yes, and it's increasingly central to what we do. We curate products made from recycled materials (like rPET), organic cotton, bamboo, cork and other responsibly sourced materials, paired with plastic-free, FSC-certified packaging where possible. Sustainability is now a baseline expectation rather than a nice-to-have, and we help you gift beautifully and responsibly.

question: Tell me about your scented products.
answer: Scent is our signature. Under IN2 Scented Art we handcraft natural candles from 100% pure soy wax — biodegradable and vegan-friendly — with exquisite fragrances developed by renowned French perfume houses, designed and produced in Europe with European-sourced ingredients. We offer candles, diffusers and home-fragrance pieces that can be fully co-branded and gift-boxed, plus corporate candle-making workshops for team-building and events.

question: What are your curated gift sets?
answer: Curated gift sets are one of our specialities: thoughtfully combined premium items — say a candle, a notebook and a bottle — presented in beautiful, cohesive packaging that feels like a retail unboxing experience. We design each set around your occasion, budget and brand, so it tells a story rather than feeling like random swag. This 'complete experience' approach is exactly where modern gifting is heading.

question: Do you design custom packaging?
answer: Yes — refined packaging is a core part of our offer, not an afterthought. We design bespoke boxes, sleeves, cards and inserts that create a memorable, retail-inspired unboxing moment, and we favour plastic-free, FSC-certified materials wherever possible. Great packaging is what turns a good product into a genuine gift.

question: Can you do co-branding?
answer: Yes — co-branding is one of our specialities. We can elegantly combine your brand with a partner's (or with our IN2 Scented Art line) so both identities feel intentional and premium rather than crowded. It's a lovely way to create collaboration gifts, event pieces or limited editions that reflect two brands with equal care.

question: What is the minimum order quantity?
answer: Minimums vary by product and branding method, because custom decoration carries setup costs (screens, tooling, print setup) that need a certain volume to make sense. Simple printed items often start around 50–100 units, while some premium or made-to-order pieces can be arranged in smaller runs. Tell us your product and quantity and we'll confirm the exact MOQ — and flag if a small-batch option is possible.

question: How long does an order take?
answer: Standard lead times are typically around 2–4 weeks after artwork approval for in-stock products with straightforward branding, while fully bespoke items, custom packaging or tooling can take longer (sometimes 8–12 weeks). If you're up against a deadline, tell us early — we can often arrange rush production or prioritise in-stock pieces. The sooner we start, the more elegant the options.

question: Can I get a sample first?
answer: Yes — for premium and bespoke projects we strongly recommend it. We can provide product samples and, for custom items, pre-production samples or prototypes so you can confirm quality, colour, branding position and finish before we commit to the full run. It's the surest way to guarantee the result feels exactly right.

question: What logo files do you need?
answer: Ideally a vector file of your logo (.AI, .EPS or vector .PDF), which scales cleanly to any size and any technique, plus your brand's Pantone (PMS) colour references so we can match your colours precisely. If you only have a raster file (like PNG or JPG), send the highest resolution you have and we'll advise — and if needed, help prepare print-ready artwork. We always send a digital proof for approval before production.

question: Do I approve a proof before production?
answer: Always. Before anything is produced we send a digital proof (a visual mockup showing your logo, colours, size and position) and refine it until you're completely happy. Nothing goes into production without your sign-off — so there are no surprises.

question: What affects the price?
answer: Several factors: the quantity (higher volumes lower the unit price), the product tier and materials, the decoration method, the number of print colours and branding positions, and any custom packaging or logistics. Premium products and refined packaging cost more per unit than throwaway swag — but they deliver far more brand value and get kept rather than binned. Share your brief and budget and we'll build the best possible mix.

question: Do more colours cost more?
answer: Yes — with methods like screen and pad printing, each additional colour and each additional print position usually adds setup and run costs. Full-colour digital and UV printing sidestep per-colour charges, which can be more economical for complex artwork. We'll recommend the branding approach that gives the best look for your budget.

question: What are the current gifting trends?
answer: The clear direction for 2025–2026 is quality over quantity — a move away from mass swag toward fewer, better, retail-worthy gifts people keep. Sustainability has become a baseline expectation, personalisation and curated 'experience' gift sets are booming, and premium tech and wellness items lead retention. Employee gifting — onboarding kits, milestones, recognition — is one of the fastest-growing use cases.

question: Is sustainable gifting important now?
answer: Very — eco-friendly promotional products are one of the fastest-growing segments, and sustainability has shifted from a differentiator to a baseline expectation. Recipients and procurement teams increasingly expect recycled or natural materials and plastic-free, responsibly sourced packaging. We help you meet that expectation with genuinely well-made sustainable products rather than 'greenwashed' throwaways.

question: Can gifts be personalised per person?
answer: Yes, and it's one of the strongest trends in gifting right now — people feel far more valued by something made for them than by generic swag. We can add individual names, roles or messages through engraving, embroidery or bespoke packaging and cards, and tailor each set to the recipient. It turns a gift into a genuine gesture.

question: What does 'retail-inspired' mean?
answer: It means gifts and packaging that look and feel like something you'd happily buy in a beautiful shop — considered design, quality materials and a memorable unboxing moment — rather than obvious corporate swag. Recipients treat retail-quality gifts as real gifts, so they keep them, use them and remember your brand. This aesthetic is central to how we design every IN2 project.

question: What occasions do you help with?
answer: All the moments that matter: client and VIP gifts, employee onboarding and welcome kits, work anniversaries and milestones, holiday and seasonal gifting, conferences, trade shows and events, product launches and co-branded collaborations. Whatever the occasion, we tailor the products, personalisation and packaging to the message you want to send.

question: Do you make onboarding / welcome kits?
answer: Yes — welcome and onboarding kits are one of our favourite projects and a fast-growing category. We curate a cohesive set — perhaps apparel, a notebook, a bottle, a candle and a personal welcome card — in beautiful branded packaging that makes new hires feel genuinely valued from day one. It's a small investment that shapes a big first impression.

question: Do you do VIP and executive gifts?
answer: Absolutely — this is where our premium, retail-inspired approach truly shines. For VIP clients and executives we design refined, personalised sets with elegant branding (think engraving and debossing) and exquisite packaging, often featuring our French-perfumed candles. The result feels like a considered luxury gift, not a giveaway.

question: Are you sustainable?
answer: Sustainability is woven through what we do. Our IN2 Scented Art candles use 100% natural, biodegradable, vegan-friendly soy wax with European-sourced ingredients, and across our range we favour recycled and natural materials with plastic-free, FSC-certified packaging where possible. We also believe the most sustainable gift is one that's well-made and kept rather than thrown away.

question: Where are your products made?
answer: We're a Polish company and, for our IN2 Scented Art candles, everything is designed and produced in Europe with all ingredients sourced from Europe. Across our wider branded range we source premium products responsibly and can prioritise EU-made and ethically sourced options when that matters to you. Just let us know your provenance requirements and we'll build the range accordingly.

question: Can you ship to my country?
answer: Yes — as a Poland-based company we serve clients across the EU and can arrange international delivery, including multi-address distribution when you're gifting to teams or clients in different locations. Share where your recipients are and your timeline, and we'll handle the logistics as part of our fully managed service. For exact options, contact Aleksandra Stan at Sales@in2branding.pl.

question: Will it follow our brand guidelines?
answer: Yes — staying true to your brand is central to how we work. Share your brand guidelines, logo files and Pantone colours, and we'll match colours precisely, apply your logo correctly and design packaging and concepts that feel unmistakably you. Every proof is sent for your approval, so you stay fully in control of how your brand appears.

question: How far can you customise?
answer: Quite far — from choosing branding techniques and colours to designing fully bespoke gift sets, custom packaging, inserts and cards, and even developing co-branded or made-to-order pieces. Some limits depend on the material and technique (for example, very fine detail doesn't suit embroidery), and truly bespoke items need more lead time. Tell us your vision and we'll tell you exactly what's possible.

question: Do you work with different budgets?
answer: We do — we'll always design the most impactful gift within your budget, whether that's a single beautifully made bottle or an elaborate curated set. What we won't do is fill a box with cheap, disposable gadgets just to hit a low price, because that undermines your brand. Share your per-recipient budget and quantity, and we'll craft options that feel premium at that level.

question: Do you offer candle-making workshops?
answer: Yes — through IN2 Scented Art we run corporate candle-making workshops, a lovely hands-on experience for team-building, client events and special occasions. Guests craft their own natural soy candle with our fine fragrances, taking home both a keepsake and the memory. To arrange one, contact us at +48 509 919 558 or Sales@in2branding.pl.
